task_completion.description: Capture a screenshot of the Sun centered in the view from Mecca at the specified time.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A screenshot of astronomy software (e.g., Stellarium) showing the Sun centered in the viewport. The location should be set to Mecca (or 21.42 N, 39.83 E) and the date/time set to May 28, 2024, 09:18:00 UTC.

task_completion.description: Capture a screenshot of the Sun centered in the view from London at the specified time.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A screenshot of astronomy software (e.g., Stellarium) showing the Sun centered in the viewport. The location should be set to London (or 51.51 N, -0.13 W) and the date/time set to May 28, 2024, 09:18:00 UTC.

task_completion.description: Create a report file named qibla_report.txt on the Desktop.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor open with a file named 'qibla_report.txt' saved in the '/home/ga/Desktop/' directory, or a file manager showing this file on the Desktop.

task_completion.description: Document the event date and time in the report.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text file contains the date 'May 28, 2024' and the time '09:18:00 UTC' (or equivalent formatting).

task_completion.description: Document the Sun's altitude as observed from Mecca in the report.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text file contains the Sun's altitude from Mecca, which should be approximately 90 degrees (or ~89.85 degrees).

task_completion.description: Document the Sun's azimuth as observed from London in the report.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text file contains the Sun's azimuth from London, which should be approximately 119 degrees (or ~118.85 degrees).

integrity.description: The agent used actual astronomy software (like Stellarium) to simulate the event.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the interface of an astronomy application being manipulated to set the location, date, and time, rather than a web browser searching for the answers.

integrity.description: The screenshots saved to the directory are genuine captures of the software.
integrity.visual_evidence: The images in ~/Pictures/stellarium/ should look like actual viewport captures from the local software, not images downloaded from the internet.

integrity.description: The agent derived the altitude and azimuth from the software rather than hallucinating or copying them.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent's trajectory should show them clicking on or targeting the Sun in the astronomy software to read its altitude and azimuth before typing those values into the text report.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected Sun altitude from Mecca at 2024-05-28 09:18:00 UTC is approximately 90 degrees (89.85°). The expected Sun azimuth from London at the same time is approximately 119 degrees (118.85°).
